session_summary:
Postgres Runtime Wiring — Layer.unwrap Fix & Desktop Startup Repair

This session addressed the DatabaseAdapter.Service layer leak that prevented createRoutes() from composing the full route graph. The root cause was InstanceLayer.layer using Layer.unwrap(Effect.promise(...)) which made the inner InstanceBootstrap dependency graph (including DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer) invisible at Layer.compile() time.

Phase A replaced the Layer.unwrap with a static import, making the full dependency graph visible. Phases B-D cleaned up the desktop startup: removed JsonMigration and node-sqlite dependencies from sidecar.ts, wired applyMigrations() into the boot sequence, renamed SQLite-specific types/IPC channels to backend-agnostic names, and made the needsMigration check OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL-aware. Phase E confirmed OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL propagation.
deferred_seams: DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer in adapter.ts also uses Layer.unwrap (documented as OOS finding)
DuckDB analytics tool path (Track 3) — not started
Prepublication review loop — deferred until checkpoint ready
Evidence artifact production — deferred until post-repair
next_steps:
1. Verify: Run `bun run typecheck` from both packages once bash access is available (expecting clean builds since changes are mechanical renames + type-equivalent transforms)
2. Validate: Run `bun test` from packages/opencode to confirm existing tests pass
3. Continue: Proceed to DuckDB analytics tool path (Seam 2) — create a standalone DuckDB tool in packages/opencode/src/tool/duckdb.ts, register in ToolRegistry
4. Continue: Extract session layout decisions from Solid components (Seam 3) — create a LayoutMode view-model to consolidate desktopV2/newLayoutDesigns branching across 15 sites
5. Long-term: Refactor DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer to remove its Layer.unwrap (OOS finding)
